props.message:
MIRELAKE.

The Source slows here, settling between the reeds. Black streams trickle in from the Burnt Hollow to the west, staining the water like old ink.
Step lightly, traveler. The peat will swallow what it can. The wisps know the path — if they don't mean you harm, follow them.
Half-sunken cairns of Source-stone, raised by older hands than ours. Lirien's name is carved on the lowest course — the rest is mud.
The shrine drowns, year by year. Whatever the keepers warded sleeps under the water now. The wisps gather thickest here.

props.open_message: An offering of the keepers, preserved in pitch and reed. A curved Source-blade that returns to the hand that throws it.
A handful of smooth river-pebbles, weighed for the sling.
A pulse of the Source, gathered into a small warm thing. It steadies your breathing.
Anchor Boots — iron-shod, drowned-keeper boots. They will pull you down where the wisps fear to follow.

root.speaker: Fisher Aelis
root.text: Quiet, quiet — the mire-fish bite shy in the bright hour. You from the plain? You here for the shrine?

shrine.speaker: Fisher Aelis
shrine.text: South of here, beneath the water. A sunken sigil-shrine. The keepers warded a thing inside; the lake has been finishing the job for a hundred summers. Bring a bright heart and a held breath.

lake.speaker: Fisher Aelis
lake.text: The Source slows here. What flows fast in the brook turns thick in the mire. It's why the wards held so long; it's why the things below are waking so slow. Slow waking is still waking.

bye.speaker: Fisher Aelis
bye.text: Tread the boards, not the peat. The peat takes.

root.speaker: Reedweaver Pell
root.text:
*braids a long reed without looking up*
Oh — visitor. Forgive me, the weave needs a steady hand. What brings you out to my island?

weave.speaker: Reedweaver Pell
weave.text: A wardmat. The reeds remember the old patterns when you set them right. Lay one in your doorway and the dim things turn aside. Of course, I have not had a customer in two seasons. *smiles*

mire.speaker: Reedweaver Pell
mire.text: Spores, mostly. Things that bloom and burst. And the great quiet beneath the water — but we don't speak of that one, not on a still day.

bye.speaker: Reedweaver Pell
bye.text: Tread softly off my island, traveler. The reeds remember footfalls.

props.open_message: On the far reed-island — only those who can walk the lakebed reach it. The drowned keepers left it for whoever could.

props.warp_message: The reeds bow. The lake will carry your weight.

root.speaker: Sunken Stone
root.text: *A faint hum, like wind through stone…*

hint_pre.speaker: Sunken Stone
hint_pre.text: The Maw lives below the lake-floor. To reach it, you must walk down.

hint_post.speaker: Sunken Stone
hint_post.text: The Boots will hold you down. The Maw will not flee.
